title: Chaos Mesh Experiment
description: Schema for Chaos Mesh chaos experiment custom resources on Kubernetes. Covers all supported fault injection types including PodChaos, NetworkChaos, IOChaos, StressChaos, HTTPChaos, TimeChaos, DNSChaos, and KernelChaos. Each chaos type specifies a selector for targeting pods and a type-specific spec for the fault to inject.

apiVersion.description: Chaos Mesh API group and version for all chaos custom resources.

kind.description: Chaos type kind identifying which fault injector to use.

spec.description: Chaos experiment specification. The required fields and their meanings vary by kind. All chaos types share selector, mode, and duration fields.

mode.description: Selection mode determining how many pods the chaos is applied to.

value.description: Numeric value for fixed, fixed-percent, or random-max-percent modes. For fixed mode, specifies exact count; for percentage modes, specifies a percentage 0-100.

duration.description: Duration of the chaos experiment in Go duration format (e.g., 30s, 5m, 1h). If omitted, the experiment runs indefinitely until manually stopped.

action.description: Specific fault action to inject. Available actions depend on the chaos kind. PodChaos supports pod-failure, pod-kill, container-kill. NetworkChaos supports delay, loss, duplicate, corrupt, partition, bandwidth.

name.description: Name of the chaos resource within its namespace.

namespace.description: Kubernetes namespace where the chaos resource will be created.

labels.description: Key-value labels for organizing and selecting chaos resources.

annotations.description: Non-identifying key-value annotations for the chaos resource.

PodSelector.description: Selector configuration for targeting specific pods for chaos injection. Multiple selection criteria can be combined.

namespaces.description: Kubernetes namespaces to select target pods from. If not specified, defaults to the namespace of the chaos resource.

labelSelectors.description: Key-value label selectors that target pods must match. All labels must be present on the pod.

expressionSelectors.description: Advanced Kubernetes label selector expressions allowing In, NotIn, Exists, and DoesNotExist operators.

annotationSelectors.description: Key-value annotation selectors that target pods must match.

fieldSelectors.description: Kubernetes field selectors for targeting pods by field values such as spec.nodeName.

podPhaseSelectors.description: Pod phases to restrict selection to, e.g., only Running pods.

nodeSelectors.description: Node label selectors to restrict which nodes' pods can be targeted.

nodes.description: Explicit list of node names to restrict pod targeting to.

pods.description: Explicit pod name lists per namespace for direct pod targeting, bypassing label selectors.

LabelSelectorRequirement.description: A Kubernetes label selector requirement for advanced pod matching.

key.description: The label key the selector applies to.

operator.description: Comparison operator for the label key.

values.description: Label values for In and NotIn operators. Must be empty for Exists and DoesNotExist.

PodChaosSpec.description: Specification for PodChaos experiments that inject failures at the pod or container level.

value.description: Value for fixed or percentage-based selection modes.

containerNames.description: Container names to target within the selected pods. Required for container-kill action.

gracePeriod.description: Grace period in seconds for pod-kill action before the pod is forcefully terminated.

NetworkChaosSpec.description: Specification for NetworkChaos experiments that inject network-level faults such as delays, packet loss, corruption, and partitions.

duration.description: Duration of the network fault injection.

direction.description: Traffic direction to affect. to affects egress; from affects ingress; both affects all traffic.

target.description: Target pod selector for partition action, defining the other side of the partition.

NetworkDelay.description: Network latency parameters for NetworkChaos delay action.

latency.description: Fixed latency to add to network packets, e.g., 100ms.

correlation.description: Correlation percentage between consecutive packets (0-100). Higher values make delays more consistent.

jitter.description: Jitter (variance) around the latency value, e.g., 10ms.

NetworkLoss.description: Network packet loss parameters for NetworkChaos loss action.

loss.description: Percentage of packets to drop, expressed as a string (0-100).

correlation.description: Correlation percentage between consecutive packet loss decisions.

NetworkBandwidth.description: Network bandwidth throttle parameters for NetworkChaos bandwidth action.

rate.description: Bandwidth throttle rate in bits per second, e.g., 1mbps.

limit.description: Number of bytes that can be queued waiting for tokens to become available.

buffer.description: Maximum number of bytes that tokens can be available for instantaneously.

StressChaosSpec.description: Specification for StressChaos experiments that inject CPU or memory stress into selected pods.

stressngStressors.description: Raw stress-ng arguments for advanced stress configuration.

containerNames.description: Container names to inject stress into. Defaults to all containers.

Stressors.description: CPU and memory stressor configuration for StressChaos.

workers.description: Number of CPU stress worker threads to spawn.

load.description: CPU utilization target as a percentage (0-100).

workers.description: Number of memory stress worker threads to spawn.

size.description: Total memory to consume per worker, e.g., 256MB or 50%.

HTTPChaosSpec.description: Specification for HTTPChaos experiments that inject faults into HTTP requests or responses.

duration.description: Duration of the HTTP chaos injection.

target.description: Whether to inject faults into HTTP Requests or Responses.

port.description: Target service port to intercept HTTP traffic on.

method.description: HTTP method filter to limit injection to specific methods.

path.description: URL path prefix or regex filter to limit injection to specific endpoints.

abort.description: When true, aborts the matching HTTP request or response.

delay.description: Latency to inject into matching HTTP interactions.

replace.description: Replacement configuration for modifying HTTP request or response content.

headers.description: Headers to replace in the request or response.

body.description: Base64-encoded body to replace the original body with.

code.description: HTTP status code to replace in the response.

patch.description: Patch configuration for modifying HTTP request or response content.

headers.description: Headers to append to the request or response.

value.description: JSON or form-encoded value to merge into the body.

Schedule.description: A Chaos Mesh Schedule resource that triggers chaos experiments automatically on a cron schedule.

ScheduleSpec.description: Specification for a Chaos Mesh Schedule resource.

schedule.description: Cron expression defining when to trigger the experiment. Supports standard cron and extended formats like @every 10m.

concurrencyPolicy.description: Policy for handling concurrent experiment instances.

historyLimit.description: Maximum number of completed experiment instances to retain in history.

startingDeadlineSeconds.description: Deadline in seconds for starting a scheduled experiment if the scheduled time was missed.

type.description: Chaos type kind for the embedded chaos experiment.
